description: Fix C-SPC (Ctrl+Space) set-mark not working on Windows Terminal / WSL2

userStories.title: Diagnose C-SPC Key Event
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to understand what key event the terminal actually sends for Ctrl+Space so I can handle it correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Add temporary debug logging to capture ev.Key() and ev.Modifiers() values for all keypresses
Run gomacs in Windows Terminal on WSL2 and press Ctrl+Space
Document what key code and modifiers are received (e.g., KeyCtrlSpace, KeyNUL, KeyRune with rune 0, etc.)
userStories.notes: tcell.KeyCtrlSpace is value 64, tcell.KeyNUL is value 0 — they are different constants. The terminal may send NUL (0x00) which could map to either, or may not send anything at all.

userStories.title: Fix Set-Mark Keybinding for Cross-Terminal Compatibility
userStories.description: As a user, I want to press C-SPC to set the mark so that I can select text regions in any terminal.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Ctrl+Space in Windows Terminal on WSL2 sets the mark and shows 'Mark set' in the message bar
Mark position matches current cursor position
Region highlighting works when moving cursor after setting mark
C-w (kill region) and M-w (copy region) work after setting mark with C-SPC
C-SPC still works on terminals where it previously worked (no regression)
userStories.notes: Based on US-001 findings, add handling for the actual key code sent by Windows Terminal. Likely need to handle KeyNUL (value 0) or KeyRune with rune 0 as additional cases alongside KeyCtrlSpace in main.go switch statement.
